content:
Member-only story

Fable 5 went dark worldwide in under an hour. Here’s the pattern that makes the vendor irrelevant

8 min read
Just now

--

On June 12, 2026, at 5:21pm Eastern, the US Commerce Department handed Anthropic an export control directive. Within hours, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were dark everywhere on Earth, for every user, regardless of nationality, because Anthropic had no way to selectively enforce a foreign-national restriction in real time across every cloud integration it shipped through.
I don’t build on Fable 5 or Mythos 5. Almost nobody outside cybersecurity research does yet. Those are the frontier cyber-defense tier, not the daily-driver models most of us wire into production. That’s exactly what made the blackout worth watching closely instead of living through directly.
Most AI resilience advice is about retries and rate limits. Retry the request, back off exponentially, cache the response, done. That’s the easy 80%. The uncomfortable 20% is the scenario almost nobody plans for because it’s never happened to them personally: the vendor itself goes away, not one request, the whole provider, with the kind of notice a government directive gives you, which is none.
